results.comment_text:
@user1:
Why is this assignment here? It's overriding the prior `self.df`
results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
def test_setitem_object_dtype_setup_does_not_overwrite_df_assignment__a1():
    path = '/workspace/asv_bench/benchmarks/indexing.py'
    source = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    cls_marker = 'class SetitemObjectDtype:'
    assert cls_marker in source, 'Expected SetitemObjectDtype benchmark class to exist in indexing.py'

def test_setitem_object_dtype_setup_does_not_overwrite_df_assignment__a2():
    path = '/workspace/asv_bench/benchmarks/indexing.py'
    source = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    cls_marker = 'class SetitemObjectDtype:'
    start = source.index(cls_marker)
    next_class = source.find('\nclass ', start + len(cls_marker))
    class_block = source[start:] if next_class == -1 else source[start:next_class]
    bad_line = 'self.df = DataFrame(np.random.rand(N, cols))'
    assert bad_line not in class_block, 'SetitemObjectDtype.setup() must not overwrite the object-dtype DataFrame with a float DataFrame'
